title: Hyppönen — MIT's $600/mo WhatsApp study, Facebook ARPU math, and why Tesla feels revolutionary
html:
If It's Smart, It's Vulnerable — Hyppönen prices the "free" services we feel entitled to: a 2017 MIT study asked users how much they'd need to be paid to give up Instagram / Twitter / Facebook / WhatsApp — WhatsApp won at $600/month. Then the ARPU math: Facebook earned ~$190 per US user/year on ads in 2020 → an ad-free, no-tracking version would only need $16/mo to break even (vs Netflix at $9). Conclusion: monetization-by-profiling is permanent. Page transitions into the Tesla chapter — Tesla reset the EV reputation (slow + ridiculous → fast + 400-mile range), and got free press by literally launching a car into space.

TL;DR

  • MIT 2017: avg user would need $600/month to give up WhatsApp — the most-valuable ostensibly-free service measured.
  • Facebook 2020 ARPU math: $190/user/yr in US ad revenue → $16/mo ad-free version would be revenue-equivalent (vs Netflix $9).
  • Tesla's rebrand of the EV from "slow / goofy" to "400-mile range / SpaceX-stunt" rewrote the playbook for product-as-marketing.

mikko hypponen · if its smart its vulnerable · tesla · masayoshi son · regret minimization framework
